description: Unified dataset for blind code review study, sourced from CodeSearchNet (real-world docstring/code pairs mined from open-source repositories). Each question is single-language (Python or Java) and contains 2 solutions: 1 human (the original CodeSearchNet function body) and 1 AI-generated. _hidden fields must never be exposed to study participants.
uiNote: Solution order must be randomized per participant and per question. Slot labels A/B are purely positional and carry no information about origin. The _hidden key on every solution object must not be rendered in any participant-facing UI component.

questions.title: Normalize Argument To List
questions.summary: Normalize Argument To List
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `normalize_listargument(arg)` that normalizes any input into a list. If `arg` is a numpy array, flatten it and convert it to a plain Python list. If `arg` is a string, wrap it in a single-element list (a string should never be treated as an iterable of characters here). If `arg` is already a list, tuple, dict, or set, convert it to a list. Otherwise, wrap `arg` in a single-element list.

questions.title: Resolve Dotted Path In Nested Dict
questions.summary: Resolve Dotted Path In Nested Dict
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `getitem_in(obj, name)` that finds a value in a nested dict `obj` via a period-delimited string `name`. For example, given `obj = {'foo': {'bar': {'baz': True}}}`, calling `getitem_in(obj, 'foo.bar.baz')` should return `True`.

questions.title: Convert Key/Value Collection To OrderedDict
questions.summary: Convert Key/Value Collection To OrderedDict
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `kvp_convert(input_coll)` that converts an iterable of strings and/or 2-tuples into a `collections.OrderedDict`. Each 2-tuple `(a, b)` becomes the mapping `a -> b`. Each plain string/scalar element becomes a mapping to `None`. If `input_coll` is already an `OrderedDict`, return it unchanged (the function should be idempotent).

questions.title: Rescale Sequence To 0-1 Range
questions.summary: Rescale Sequence To 0-1 Range
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `makeSequenceRelative(absVSequence)` that rescales every value in a numeric list onto a continuum between 0 and 1 (min maps to 0, max maps to 1). It should return a 3-tuple: `(relative_sequence, min_value, max_value)`, where the min/max are also returned so the transform can later be reversed. If the sequence has fewer than two elements, or every element is identical (so there's no range to normalize against), raise an exception.

solutions.code:
def makeSequenceRelative(absVSequence):
    if len(absVSequence) < 2 or len(set(absVSequence)) == 1:
        raise RelativizeSequenceException(absVSequence)

    minV = min(absVSequence)
    maxV = max(absVSequence)
    relativeSeq = [(value - minV) / (maxV - minV) for value in absVSequence]

    return relativeSeq, minV, maxV

questions.title: Map Array Indices To Elements
questions.summary: Map Array Indices To Elements
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `index_element_map(arr)` that takes a list `arr` and returns a dictionary mapping each index in the list to the element found at that index.

solutions.code:
def index_element_map(arr):
    return {index: element for index, element in enumerate(arr)}

questions.title: Strip Terminal Escape Characters
questions.summary: Strip Terminal Escape Characters
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `strip_esc_sequence(txt)` that replaces every ESC control character (ASCII 27) in a string with a `?` character, to prevent untrusted strings from injecting their own terminal manipulation sequences. If `txt` is not a string, return it unchanged.

questions.title: Convert Dict To Comma-Separated Settings String
questions.summary: Convert Dict To Comma-Separated Settings String
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `dictionary_to_stringlist(input_dict)` that converts a dictionary into a comma-separated settings string. For example, the dictionary `{'setting1': 'value1', 'setting2': 'value2'}` should become the string `'setting1=value1,setting2=value2'` (no trailing comma).

solutions.code:
def dictionary_to_stringlist(input_dict):
    return ",".join("{0}={1}".format(key, value) for key, value in input_dict.items())

questions.title: Recursively Merge Two Mappings
questions.summary: Recursively Merge Two Mappings
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `merge_map(a, b)` that recursively merges `b` into `a`. If both `a` and `b` are lists, return their concatenation. If both are dicts, recursively merge each key from `b` into `a` (merging further if both values are mergeable, otherwise `b`'s value overwrites `a`'s), with `b` taking precedence on conflicts. If `a` and `b` are not both lists or both dicts, simply return `b`.

questions.title: Convert Argument To Reddit-Formatted List
questions.summary: Convert Argument To Reddit-Formatted List
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `to_reddit_list(arg)` that returns an argument converted to a reddit-formatted list: a comma-delimited string where each element is the string representation of an object. If `arg` is a string, or is not iterable/indexable at all, just return its string representation directly (don't try to iterate over the characters of a string). Otherwise, treat `arg` as an iterable of items and join their string representations with commas.

questions.title: Format Decimal Value For Display
questions.summary: Format Decimal Value For Display
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `format_value(value)` that returns `value` rounded to one decimal place. If the value is a whole number (has no fractional part), return it as a plain `int`. Otherwise, return it as a `float` rounded to one decimal place.

questions.title: Compute Identity Card Check Digit
questions.summary: Compute Identity Card Check Digit
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `checksum_identity_card_number(characters)` that computes the check digit for a Polish Identity Card Number. `characters` is a 9-element sequence: the first 3 elements are uppercase letters and the remaining 6 are digits. Each of the first 3 letters is converted to a number via `ord(letter) - 55` (so `'A'` -> 10, `'B'` -> 11, etc.), and each of the remaining 6 elements is used as-is as a digit. Multiply each of the 9 values by the weights `[7, 3, 1, 0, 7, 3, 1, 7, 3]`, sum the products, and return that sum modulo 10.

solutions.code:
def checksum_identity_card_number(characters):
    weights = [7, 3, 1, 0, 7, 3, 1, 7, 3]
    total = sum(weights[i] * (ord(characters[i]) - 55) for i in range(3))
    total += sum(weights[i] * characters[i] for i in range(4, 9))
    return total % 10

questions.title: Compute Misclassification Cost
questions.summary: Compute Misclassification Cost
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `calc_cost(y, yhat, cost_matrix)` that computes the average misclassification cost given ground-truth labels `y`, predicted labels `yhat`, and a `cost_matrix` where `cost_matrix[i][j]` is the cost of the ground truth being class `i` but predicted as class `j`. `y` and `yhat` are equal-length sequences of class indices, and `cost_matrix` is a 2D numpy array. Return the mean cost over all predictions.

solutions.code:
def calc_cost(y, yhat, cost_matrix):
    return np.mean(cost_matrix[list(y), list(yhat)])

questions.title: Combine Two Limit Ranges
questions.summary: Combine Two Limit Ranges
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `find_minmax(lims, olims)` that takes two 2-tuples `(a1, a2)` and `(b1, b2)` representing value ranges and returns the combined range `(nanmin(a1, b1), nanmax(a2, b2))`, ignoring NaNs, so it can be used to expand plotting limits across multiple items. If the inputs can't be compared (e.g. incompatible types), return `(NaN, NaN)` instead of raising.

questions.title: Truncate String With Ellipsis
questions.summary: Truncate String With Ellipsis
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `truncate(string, maxchar)` that truncates `string` to at most `maxchar` characters. If `string` is longer than `maxchar`, cut it down and append `'...'` so the result is exactly `maxchar` characters long. `maxchar` must be at least 4; raise an error if it is smaller. If `string` already fits, return it unchanged.

questions.title: Find MST Containing A Stroke Index
questions.summary: Find MST Containing A Stroke Index
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `find_split_node(mst_wood, i)` that searches a list of minimum-spanning-tree dicts `mst_wood` (each with a `'strokes'` list) for the one containing stroke index `i`, and returns a tuple `(mst_index, node_index)`: the index of the matching MST within `mst_wood`, and the index of `i` within that MST's `'strokes'` list. Raise `ValueError` if `i` isn't found in any MST.

questions.title: Deterministically Format A Dictionary
questions.summary: Deterministically Format A Dictionary
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `stable_format_dict(d)` that formats a dictionary as a `{key: value, ...}` string, sorted by key, so the output is stable/deterministic across Python 2 and 3. Keys should be formatted with `repr`, except that a leading `u` prefix from `repr` of a unicode string key (e.g. `u'foo'`) should be stripped so it renders as `'foo'`.

questions.title: Decode Base64-Encoded Int Array
questions.summary: Decode Base64-Encoded Int Array
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `decode_ints(message)` that decodes a base64-encoded array of little-endian 32-bit integers. `message` is a base64-encoded string; base64-decode it first, then unpack the resulting bytes as a sequence of little-endian 4-byte signed integers, and return them.

questions.title: Convert Timedelta To Whole Minutes
questions.summary: Convert Timedelta To Whole Minutes
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `timedelta_total_minutes(td, strict=False)` that converts/rounds a `datetime.timedelta` to a whole number of minutes. If `td` is not an exact multiple of 60 seconds: when `strict` is `True`, raise a `ValueError`; otherwise round to the nearest minute (rounding up on a tie, i.e. exactly 30 seconds or more rounds up).

questions.title: Build Boolean Mask Array From Index Lists
questions.summary: Build Boolean Mask Array From Index Lists
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `masks(list_of_index_lists, n)` that builds a 2D boolean array where each row is a length-`n` boolean mask. `list_of_index_lists` is a list of lists of indices; for each inner list, produce a length-`n` boolean array that is `True` at exactly the given indices and `False` elsewhere, and stack all of these rows into a single array.

solutions.code:
def masks(list_of_index_lists, n):
    for il,l in enumerate(list_of_index_lists):
        mask = np.zeros(n,dtype=bool)
        mask[l] = True
        list_of_index_lists[il] = mask
    masks = np.array(list_of_index_lists)
    return masks

questions.title: Recursively Remove Empty Values From Dict
questions.summary: Recursively Remove Empty Values From Dict
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `scrub_dict(d)` that recursively removes empty values from a dictionary: empty strings, empty lists, empty dicts, `None`, etc. It should recurse into nested dicts and lists too, dropping empty values at every level, and return the cleaned structure. Non-dict, non-list values should be returned unchanged.

questions.title: Sort A Dict By Key Or Value
questions.summary: Sort A Dict By Key Or Value
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `rank_dict(dict, key=lambda t: t[1], as_tuple=False)` that sorts a dict by a given key function (sorting by value by default), and returns the result as an `OrderedDict`. If `as_tuple` is `True`, return the sorted `(key, value)` pairs as a tuple instead of an `OrderedDict`.

questions.title: Decode Byte Array Into UUID List
questions.summary: Decode Byte Array Into UUID List
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `bytes_to_uuid_list(byte_array)` that converts a byte array into a list of UUID objects. The byte array's length is a multiple of 16; every consecutive 16-byte chunk should be parsed as one UUID.

questions.title: Compute ISBN-13 Check Digit
questions.summary: Compute ISBN-13 Check Digit
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `get_isbn13_checksum(isbn)` that computes the final ISBN-13 check digit. `isbn` is the first 12 digits, given as a list of single-digit integers. Multiply the digits alternately by 1 and 3 (i.e. weights `1,3,1,3,...`), sum the products, take the sum modulo 10, and return `10 - (sum % 10)` unless that remainder is 0, in which case return 0.

solutions.code:
def get_isbn13_checksum(isbn):
    digits = [int(d) for d in isbn]
    weights = [1, 3] * 6
    rest = sum(w * d for w, d in zip(weights, digits)) % 10
    return 0 if rest == 0 else 10 - rest

questions.title: Find Return Target Index In Call Stack
questions.summary: Find Return Target Index In Call Stack
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `find_return_target(stack, target)` that checks if `target` (a return address) exists in `stack`, a list of call-stack frame objects, each with a `ret_addr` attribute. Search from the start of `stack` (the most recent frame) and return the index of the first frame whose `ret_addr` equals `target`, or `None` if no frame matches.

solutions.code:
def find_return_target(stack, target):
    return next((i for i, frame in enumerate(stack) if frame.ret_addr == target), None)

questions.title: Decode Encoded Centroids Using Label Mappings
questions.summary: Decode Encoded Centroids Using Label Mappings
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `decode_centroids(encoded, mapping)` that decodes an encoded 2D centroids array back into its original data labels. `encoded` is a 2D numpy array where `encoded[:, col]` holds integer-encoded values for feature `col`. `mapping` is a list of dicts (one per column), where `mapping[col]` maps an original label to its integer code. Return a 2D array of the same shape as `encoded`, with every encoded value replaced by its original label (i.e., invert each column's mapping and apply it element-wise).

questions.title: Collect Distinct Column Keys From Nested Dict
questions.summary: Collect Distinct Column Keys From Nested Dict
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `filter_data_columns(data)` that takes a dict whose values are themselves dicts of column-name to count (the keys of the outer dict don't matter for this), and returns a sorted list of every distinct column name that appears across all of the inner dicts.

questions.title: Count Key Occurrences In Nested Dict
questions.summary: Count Key Occurrences In Nested Dict
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `countKey(theDict, name)` that counts how many times the key `name` appears anywhere in a nested dictionary tree (`theDict` may contain dicts as values, nested to any depth). Recurse into every nested dict and sum up all matches at every level.

questions.title: Find Index Where Brackets Balance
questions.summary: Find Index Where Brackets Balance
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `find_balanced(text, start, open_c, close_c)` that, starting at index `start` (which is assumed to be right after one unmatched `open_c`), scans forward through `text` and returns the index right after the `open_c`/`close_c` character counts balance out (i.e. once every extra `open_c` seen has been matched by a `close_c`). If the end of the string is reached first, return the string's length.

questions.title: Transpose And Average Collected Stats
questions.summary: Transpose And Average Collected Stats
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `iostats_dict(header, stats)` that transposes collected I/O statistics and averages them. `stats` is a list of samples, where each sample is a list of `decimal.Decimal` readings (one per header, in the same order as `header`). Group the readings by position (i.e., by header) across all samples, average each group, round to 2 decimal places, and return a dict mapping each entry of `header` to its rounded average `float` value.

solutions.code:
def iostats_dict(header, stats):
    stats = [float((sum(stat) / len(stat)).quantize(decimal.Decimal('.01'))) for stat in zip(*stats)]
    stats = dict(zip(header, stats))
    return stats

questions.title: Extract Comma-Separated Tag=Value Pairs
questions.summary: Extract Comma-Separated Tag=Value Pairs
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a function `label_list_parser(url)` that extracts comma-separated `tag=value` pairs from a string. Assume every character other than `/` and `,` is a valid part of a tag or value. Return the set of matched `tag=value` substrings (an empty match should be excluded from the result).

solutions.code:
def label_list_parser(url):
    pairs = set(re.findall(r'[^/,]+=[^/,]+', url))
    pairs.discard('')
    return pairs

questions.title: Convert Tag List To Sorted Map
questions.summary: Convert Tag List To Sorted Map
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a generic method `toSortedMap(List<Tag> tags, Comparator<String> comparator)` that converts a list of `Tag` objects (each with a `getName()` method) into a `Map<String, Tag>` keyed by tag name. If `comparator` is non-null, use it to order the map's keys (e.g. via a `TreeMap`); if `comparator` is `null`, preserve the tags' original list order (e.g. via a `LinkedHashMap`).

questions.title: Apply Partial String Replacements From A Map
questions.summary: Apply Partial String Replacements From A Map
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `applyReplacements(CharSequence word, LinkedHashMap<String, String> replacements)` that applies each `from -> to` replacement in `replacements` (in iteration order) to `word`, replacing every non-overlapping occurrence of `from` with `to`, and returns the resulting string.

questions.title: Collapse Whitespace In A Token
questions.summary: Collapse Whitespace In A Token
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `trimWhitespace(String s)` that filters whitespace out of a token string: trim leading/trailing whitespace first, then drop newline/tab/carriage-return characters entirely, and drop any *run* of two or more consecutive whitespace characters entirely too. The one exception: a single, isolated whitespace character (bordered by non-whitespace on both sides) is kept as-is — e.g. so a number formatted with a single space as a separator, like `"12 345"`, stays `"12 345"`, while `"a  b"` (two spaces) becomes `"ab"`.

questions.title: Convert Digest Bytes To Hex String
questions.summary: Convert Digest Bytes To Hex String
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `checksumBytesToString(byte[] digestBytes)` that converts a message digest byte array into its lowercase hexadecimal string representation (two hex characters per byte, zero-padded).

questions.title: Count Children Of A Parent In Dependency Tree
questions.summary: Count Children Of A Parent In Dependency Tree
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `countChildrenOf(int[] parents, int parent)` that counts the number of children in a dependency tree for the given parent index. `parents` is an array where `parents[i]` contains the index of the parent of the word at position `i`. Return the number of positions `i` for which `parents[i] == parent`.

questions.title: Find First Index Of Any Character
questions.summary: Find First Index Of Any Character
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `indexOneOf(CharSequence string, char... chars)` that returns the index of the first character in `string` that matches any character in `chars`, scanning left to right. Return -1 if none of the characters occur.

questions.title: Format Map As Sorted Key-Value String
questions.summary: Format Map As Sorted Key-Value String
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `listToString(Map<?, ?> map)` that lists the content of a map as a string, one entry per line, sorted by key, with each line formatted as `"key\t= value\n"`. Null keys or values should be rendered as the literal text `"null"`. If the map itself is `null`, return the string `"null"`.

questions.title: Compute Checkpointed Sequence And Garbage-Collect
questions.summary: Compute Checkpointed Sequence And Garbage-Collect
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `getCheckpointedSequence()` on a class that tracks a sequence of removable values, with these existing instance fields: `lastSequence` (a `long`, the last sequence number ever added), `sequences` (a `TreeSet<Long>` of sequence numbers for values not yet garbage-collected), `firstValueSequence` (a `long`, the sequence number corresponding to `values.get(0)`), and `values` (a `List` in sequence order starting at `firstValueSequence`). The method must return the maximum consecutively-removed sequence number: if `sequences` is empty that's `lastSequence`, otherwise it's `sequences.first() - 1` (one less than the smallest still-pending sequence number). Before returning, garbage-collect: remove entries from the front of `values` for every sequence number up through the checkpoint, and advance `firstValueSequence` by however many were removed.

questions.title: Parse Comma-Separated Long Array
questions.summary: Parse Comma-Separated Long Array
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `parseLongArray(String source)` that parses a comma-separated list of numbers (e.g. `"25, 17125141422, 21, 99"`, whitespace around each number is allowed) into a `long[]`. If any token fails to parse as a long, return `null` instead of throwing.

questions.title: Wrap Map Values Into Singleton Lists
questions.summary: Wrap Map Values Into Singleton Lists
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `toMultivaluedMap(Map<String, Object> seed)` that converts a `Map<String, Object>` into a `Map<String, List<Object>>` by wrapping each value in a single-element, read-only list. Throw a `NullPointerException` if `seed` is `null`.

questions.title: Filter Map Values By Key List
questions.summary: Filter Map Values By Key List
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `filterMapToList(Map<String, ?> map, String[] nameMapping)` that returns a `List<Object>` of all the values in `map` whose key matches an entry in `nameMapping`, in the same order as `nameMapping` (so the result has one entry per name, possibly `null` if the key isn't present in `map`). Throw a `NullPointerException` if either `map` or `nameMapping` is `null`.

questions.title: Compare Two Time-Based UUIDs
questions.summary: Compare Two Time-Based UUIDs
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `compare(UUID uuid1, UUID uuid2)` that deterministically compares two time-based (version 1) UUIDs: first by their embedded timestamp, then by their node-specific clock sequence number, and finally by their embedded node identifier, breaking ties at each step in that order. Both UUIDs are guaranteed to be time-based.

questions.title: Read Null-Terminated String From Byte Array
questions.summary: Read Null-Terminated String From Byte Array
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `getString(byte[] data, int offset)` that reads a string of single-byte characters from `data`, starting at `offset`. The string ends either at the end of the array, or when a zero byte is encountered (whichever comes first) — the terminating zero byte itself is not included in the result.

questions.title: Trim Array To A Maximum Capacity
questions.summary: Trim Array To A Maximum Capacity
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `trimToCapacity(short[] array, int maxCapacity)` that ensures the given array holds no more than `maxCapacity` elements. If `array.length <= maxCapacity`, return the array unchanged (the identical reference). Otherwise, return a new array of length `maxCapacity` containing the first `maxCapacity` elements of the original.

questions.title: Compute Trace Of A Sparse Matrix
questions.summary: Compute Trace Of A Sparse Matrix
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `trace(DMatrixSparseCSC A)` that computes the trace of a sparse matrix stored in compressed sparse column (CSC) format: `trace = sum(A[i][i] for i in 0..n)` where `n = min(A.numRows, A.numCols)`. The matrix exposes these fields: `numRows`, `numCols`, `col_idx` (an `int[]` of length `numCols + 1`, where nonzero entries of column `c` occupy indices `[col_idx[c], col_idx[c+1])` in the parallel arrays below), `nz_rows` (an `int[]` of row indices for each nonzero entry), and `nz_values` (a `double[]` of the corresponding nonzero values).

questions.title: Safely Parse A String To Integer
questions.summary: Safely Parse A String To Integer
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `getInteger(String value)` that parses `value` into an `Integer`, silently returning `null` if `value` is `null` or cannot be parsed as an integer, instead of throwing.

questions.title: Convert Object List To String Array
questions.summary: Convert Object List To String Array
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `objectListToStringArray(List<?> objectList)` that converts a list of objects into a `String[]` by calling `toString()` on each non-null element (null elements map to `null`). If `objectList` itself is `null`, return `null`.

questions.title: Compute Accuracy From A Confusion Matrix
questions.summary: Compute Accuracy From A Confusion Matrix
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `computeAccuracy()` on a class that holds a confusion matrix `matrix` (with `matrix.get(row, col)` giving the count for that cell) and an array `actualCounts` whose length is the number of distinct classes/types. Compute overall accuracy as the number of correctly classified samples (the sum of the diagonal cells) divided by the total number of samples (the sum of every cell in the matrix).

questions.title: Compute Ratio Of Smallest To Largest Value
questions.summary: Compute Ratio Of Smallest To Largest Value
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `ratioSmallestOverLargest(double[] sv)` that computes the ratio of the smallest value in the array to the largest value, without assuming the array is sorted. Return `Double.NaN` if the array is empty.

questions.title: Transform Each Element Of A Multiset
questions.summary: Transform Each Element Of A Multiset
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a generic method `<A, B> ImmutableMultiset<B> transformedCopy(Multiset<A> ms, Function<A, B> func)` that returns a new `Multiset` (Guava's collection type that tracks a count per distinct element) resulting from applying `func` to each distinct element of `ms`, preserving each element's count. If two or more elements are mapped to the same value by `func`, their counts should be summed in the result.

questions.title: Compute Overflow-Safe L2 Norm Of A Counter
questions.summary: Compute Overflow-Safe L2 Norm Of A Counter
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a generic method `<E, C extends Counter<E>> double saferL2Norm(C c)` (where `Counter` supports `keySet()` returning its keys, and `getCount(key)` returning that key's associated double value) that computes the L2 norm of the counter's values in a numerically safer way, for counters that may hold very large values: first find the maximum absolute value among all entries, then compute `maxVal * sqrt(sum((value / maxVal)^2 for each value))`. Scaling by the max before squaring avoids the overflow that summing squares of very large values directly could cause.

questions.title: Format Text For CSV Output
questions.summary: Format Text For CSV Output
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `csv(String text)` that formats text for a CSV field: trim whitespace, replace newlines with spaces, and then, if the result contains a comma, double quote, or would otherwise need escaping, wrap it in double quotes and double up any internal double quotes. If `text` is `null` or blank, return `"\"\""` (an empty quoted field).

questions.title: Check If Any Search Character Is Present
questions.summary: Check If Any Search Character Is Present
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `containsAny(char[] input, char[] searchChars)` that returns `true` if any character in `input` also appears in `searchChars`. `input` may be `null` (return `false` in that case); `searchChars` is never `null`.

questions.title: Check For DELETE Keyword Case-Insensitively
questions.summary: Check For DELETE Keyword Case-Insensitively
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `parseDeleteKeyword(char[] query, int offset)` that checks, case-insensitively, whether the 6 characters of `query` starting at `offset` spell out `"DELETE"`. Return `false` if there aren't at least 6 characters remaining in `query` from `offset` onward.

questions.title: Encode A Number Into A Fixed-Length Byte Range
questions.summary: Encode A Number Into A Fixed-Length Byte Range
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `encodeNum(long num, byte[] data, int beginIndex, int maxBytes)` that encodes `num` into `maxBytes` big-endian bytes and writes them into `data` starting at `beginIndex`. Return the index in `data` immediately after the bytes that were written (`beginIndex + maxBytes`).

questions.title: Find Index Of Element By Reference Identity
questions.summary: Find Index Of Element By Reference Identity
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a generic method `<T> int getIndex(List<T> l, T o)` that returns the index of the first occurrence of `o` in list `l`, using reference identity (`==`) rather than `.equals(...)` as the presence test. Return `-1` if `l` does not contain `o`.

questions.title: Parse Comma-Separated Float Array
questions.summary: Parse Comma-Separated Float Array
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `parseFloatArray(String source)` that parses a comma-separated list of numbers (e.g. `"25.0, .5, 1, 0.99"`, whitespace around each number is allowed) into a `float[]`. If any token fails to parse as a float, return `null` instead of throwing.

questions.title: Compute Slippy Map Tile Number
questions.summary: Compute Slippy Map Tile Number
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `getTileNumber(double lat, double lon, int zoom)` that computes the OpenStreetMap "slippy map" tile numbers `[zoom, xtile, ytile]` for a given latitude, longitude and zoom level, using the standard Mercator tile formula. Clamp both `xtile` and `ytile` into the valid range `[0, 2^zoom - 1]` in case rounding pushes them just outside the extent.

questions.title: Calculate Total Length Of Bind Placeholders
questions.summary: Calculate Total Length Of Bind Placeholders
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a method `calculateBindLength(int bindCount)` that calculates the total text length needed to represent `bindCount` PostgreSQL bind-variable placeholders (`$1`, `$2`, ..., `$10`, `$11`, ...), accounting for the fact that placeholder numbers with more digits take up more characters (`$1`..`$9` are 2 characters each, `$10`..`$99` are 3 characters each, and so on).

questions.title: Sort Map Entries By Key
questions.summary: Sort Map Entries By Key
questions.paraphrasedPrompt: Write a generic method `<K extends Comparable<? super K>, V> List<Map.Entry<K, V>> sortedEntries(Collection<Map.Entry<K, V>> entries)` that returns the given collection of map entries as a `List`, sorted in ascending order by key.
